Growth Analytics Lead
 Job Reference twinkl/TP/661/1530

 Number of Positions: 1
 Contract Type: Full Time
 Salary: Competitive
 Working Hours: 37.5
 Location: Remote - UK
 Closing Date: 25/04/2026
 Job Category: Data
 Region / Division: Not Applicable
 Business Unit: Not Applicable
Job Introduction
Growth Analytics Lead



Location: Remote UK

Contract: Permanent 

Hours: 37.5 hours (Core hours 10:00am - 2:00pm Monday - Friday) 





We're here to help those who teach. It's what brings us to work every day. At Twinkl, we’ve been providing educators across the world with high quality, trusted teaching and learning resources for over a decade now. They’re all written and checked by our wonderful team of experienced educators, and there are hundreds of thousands of resources to download, with new ones added daily.



We are looking for a Growth Analytics Lead to own measurement, experimentation, and decision support across the subscriber growth lifecycle. This role will define how we measure and optimise acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and lifetime value, ensuring growth investments deliver strong long-term returns.



You will partner closely with Growth, Product, Marketing, Finance, and Data teams to identify opportunities, run experiments, and translate insights into actions that drive sustainable subscriber growth.



This is a hands-on leadership role, responsible for leading growth analytics and building scalable measurement frameworks for a fast-growing subscription business.





Role Description & Responsibilities:

Own Growth Measurement & Insights:

Define and scale measurement frameworks across the full subscriber lifecycle, including acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and monetisation.

Deliver insights that inform growth strategy, investment decisions, and product improvements.



Support a Culture of Experimentation:

Lead the design and execution of experimentation frameworks across marketing and lifecycle channels.

Design and analyse A/B tests, holdout experiments, and incrementality tests.

Ensure experimentation results translate into clear decisions and growth initiatives.



Lead analytics delivery across Growth marketing, Brand, Markets & Product marketing, including:

SEO and Performance Marketing reporting

Brand performance measurement

Market-level growth analysis

Demand generation insights



Optimise Marketing & Growth Economics:

Measure and improve customer acquisition efficiency and subscriber quality.

Evaluate marketing performance using CAC, ROAS and incremental ROAS.

Provide recommendations for budget allocation, channel strategy, and growth investment.



Drive Lifecycle & Retention Analytics:

Analyse subscriber behaviour across the lifecycle to identify drivers of engagement, retention and churn.

Evaluate the impact of CRM programs, lifecycle messaging, segmentation and personalisation.

Identify opportunities to improve activation, engagement and long-term subscriber value.



Generate Customer & Subscriber Insight:

Develop a deep understanding of customer behaviour using cohort analysis, segmentation and behavioural analytics.

Build frameworks for customer profiling and subscriber segmentation.

Improve measurement through identity resolution and cross-channel attribution.



Build & Lead a High-Impact Analytics Team:

Manage and develop high performing analysts.

Set standards for analytical rigor, data quality, documentation and reproducibility.

Partner with Data Engineering to ensure reliable data pipelines and instrumentation.



Qualifications:

6+ years experience in Growth, Marketing, or Product Analytics.

3+ years experience leading analytics teams or managing analysts.

Strong experience analysing customer acquisition and lifecycle performance in digital businesses.

Advanced SQL and experience with Python or R for statistical analysis.

Strong experience designing and analysing experiments and causal measurement frameworks.

Experience building dashboards and reporting using BI tools such as Tableau or Looker.

Proven ability to translate complex analysis into clear business recommendations and decisions.

Experience working in subscription or recurring revenue businesses.

Strong understanding of subscription unit economics, including CAC, LTV, payback period, and churn.

Experience analysing large-scale consumer platforms with multiple acquisition channels and markets.

Experience working with product analytics platforms such as GA4

Familiarity with modern analytics engineering practices such as dbt, Git, and data quality monitoring.

Experience partnering closely with product and growth teams in experimentation-driven environments.



If you require a reasonable adjustment to the application/selection process to enable you to demonstrate your ability to perform the job requirements please include this at the foot of your covering letter. This will help us to understand any modifications we may need to make to support you throughout our selection process.





Benefits:

In return for everything you can bring, we can offer you an exciting role in a fast-growing and dynamic business, with plenty of career opportunities.

A friendly, welcoming and supportive culture. We believe work should be fun and always put people before the process

Diversity, inclusion and belonging - our Employee Network Program includes working groups for LGBTQ+, People of Colour, Disabilities (visible and invisible), Women in Tech and Working Parents.

From day 1 - Westfield Health, 33 annual leave days per year (pro-rata) inclusive bank holidays, a "Me" day each year, a charity day each year, flexible working policy with opportunities to work from home and Twinkl subscriptions.

Quarterly company awards programme

Seasonal events

Referral scheme

Cervical and Prostate screening

Company sick pay after 3 months of service 

After probation - cycle-to-work scheme

Long-term service reward - Life insurance, enhanced pension contribution, enhanced maternity pay, enhanced adoption pay and enhanced paternity pay, long service award, long service annual leave

Ben AutoFill
shortCompanyReason: Twinkl provides teaching and learning resources for educators worldwide.
shortRoleReason: Lead growth analytics across measurement, experimentation, and subscriber growth.
companySummary: Twinkl is an education company that creates trusted teaching and learning resources for educators worldwide.
roleSummary: The role leads growth analytics for a subscription business, covering measurement, experimentation, lifecycle analysis, and team leadership.
headlineAttraction: Lead growth analytics for a fast-growing subscription business with broad ownership and team leadership.
rolePurpose: To define and improve how Twinkl measures and optimises subscriber growth across acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, and lifetime value.
advertSummary: Twinkl is hiring a remote UK Growth Analytics Lead to own growth measurement, experimentation, and decision support across the subscriber lifecycle. The role partners with Growth, Product, Marketing, Finance, and Data teams, leads analytics delivery across marketing and product areas, and manages analysts in a fast-growing subscription business.
personalisedIntro: I’m applying because this role sits right at the point where measurement, decision-making and delivery meet. My background is in taking messy operational environments, building better reporting and turning that into practical action. That’s the kind of work I’d expect to do well here.
whyThisRole: The work itself is a strong fit for how I operate: define what matters, build the reporting around it, and use that to improve performance. I’ve spent my career in roles where I had to bring structure to live operations, improve service quality, and give leaders better information to act on. This feels like a chance to apply that same discipline to subscriber growth.
coreResponsibilities: Deliver marketing and brand performance analytics
Analyse acquisition efficiency and subscriber quality
Assess CAC, ROAS, and incremental ROAS
Analyse retention, churn, and lifecycle messaging
Build customer profiling and segmentation frameworks
Set standards for rigor, documentation, and reproducibility
Partner with Data Engineering on pipelines and instrumentation
essentialRequirements: 6+ years in Growth, Marketing, or Product Analytics
3+ years leading analytics teams or managing analysts
Strong customer acquisition and lifecycle analytics experience
Python or R for statistical analysis
BI dashboarding experience with Tableau or Looker
Ability to translate analysis into business recommendations
Experience in subscription or recurring revenue businesses
Understanding of CAC, LTV, payback period, and churn
Familiarity with dbt, Git, and data quality monitoring
Experience partnering with product and growth teams
senioritySignals: manage and develop high performing analysts

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operations leader who turns complex data into clearer decisions and better outcomes.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m applying because this role sits right at the point where measurement, decision-making and delivery meet. My background is in taking messy operational environments, building better reporting and turning that into practical action. That’s the kind of work I’d expect to do well here.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Twinkl stands out because it is built around trusted resources for educators, with a clear focus on quality and scale. I also notice the emphasis on a supportive, welcoming culture and on people before process, which matters to me in a remote role where trust and clarity count.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself is a strong fit for how I operate: define what matters, build the reporting around it, and use that to improve performance. I’ve spent my career in roles where I had to bring structure to live operations, improve service quality, and give leaders better information to act on. This feels like a chance to apply that same discipline to subscriber growth.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I can turn fragmented operational data into something leaders can actually use. That is close to the kind of measurement clarity this role needs across growth and lifecycle performance.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting example for reporting, dashboard thinking and decision support.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve built reporting that brings scattered operational signals into one clear view.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: performance improvement through insight and action
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I can improve a live customer outcome, not just report on it. The scale of the turnaround also shows I’m comfortable working with performance data where the result matters.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a proof point for turning insight into measurable improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve taken a poor customer signal and helped turn it into a much stronger result.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: operational control in a fast-growing environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I chose this because it shows I’ve worked in a business scaling under pressure, where delivery discipline and resilience mattered. That is relevant to a fast-growing subscription environment where growth has to be measured and controlled properly.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a credibility example for pace, control and commercial awareness.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve helped tighten delivery and resilience while a business was growing.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can make growth measurement useful, not just technically sound. It also needs someone who can work across teams, challenge assumptions with evidence, and keep the analytics practical enough to shape investment, retention and experimentation decisions.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built reporting that pulls together fragmented operational data so leaders can see what is happening and where pressure is building.
experienceMappings.relevance: That same approach is useful when measuring acquisition, activation, retention and value across a lifecycle.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I haven’t claimed formal A/B testing ownership in my CV, but I have repeatedly worked in environments where I had to test changes, watch the impact and adjust quickly based on evidence.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs disciplined experimentation, so I would bring a practical, evidence-led approach and build from there.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve improved a customer-facing outcome by tightening operational follow-through and using the data to focus attention on what was not working.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I can work from performance signals to action, which is central to growth and marketing analysis.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked on improving repeat customer experience in a live service setting, where consistency and follow-through affected the result.
experienceMappings.relevance: Retention work depends on understanding what keeps people engaged and what causes drop-off.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led day-to-day operations and coordinated people, reporting and delivery in environments where standards had to hold under pressure.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role needs someone who can manage analysts and set a clear bar for quality.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked across production, people, systems and customer needs in a scale-up setting, and I’m used to aligning different priorities around a shared result.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role depends on working well with Growth, Product, Marketing, Finance and Data.

focusAreasToBring.title: Clear measurement
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making the growth picture easier to trust and easier to use, so decisions are based on consistent numbers rather than competing views.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical experimentation
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep experiments tied to real business questions, so the output is not just analysis but a clear recommendation on what to do next.

focusAreasToBring.title: Operational discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would bring a strong habit of structure, documentation and follow-through, because analytics only helps if people can rely on it.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background is not a direct copy of this role, but the underlying pattern is a strong match: I’ve spent years improving performance in complex environments by building better reporting, tightening control and turning data into action. I’m comfortable working across functions, and I’m used to being accountable for outcomes rather than just analysis.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend my first months getting close to the current measurement setup, understanding where the growth numbers are trusted and where they are not, and identifying the biggest gaps in lifecycle insight. From there, I’d focus on making experimentation more useful, improving the quality of decision support, and helping the team produce analysis that is easier to act on.
companyHighlights: Twinkl provides trusted teaching and learning resources for educators worldwide.
The advert signals a supportive, welcoming culture with flexibility and a people-first approach.
The role sits in a fast-growing subscription business with broad ownership across growth analytics.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people want honest analysis, clear priorities and a calm approach to fixing problems. The mix of flexibility, support and trust in Twinkl’s culture signals a setting where I’d be able to do my best work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the existing growth metrics, reporting logic, stakeholder expectations and data quality issues so I can see where the biggest measurement gaps are.
I would focus on the most important lifecycle, marketing and retention questions, and start tightening how insights are framed so they lead to clearer decisions.
I would look to strengthen repeatable reporting, experiment review and analyst ways of working so the team has a more reliable base to build from.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring a steady, evidence-led approach to helping Twinkl make better growth decisions and build stronger analytics discipline.
closingProofPoints: I improved a live NHS customer outcome from a 2.09 to 4.51 Google review score.
I built reporting that reconciled 64 PCSE remittance files covering £2.83m.
I helped strengthen operational performance during a manufacturing scale-up phase.
contentNotes: I have not invented direct experience with GA4, Tableau, Looker, dbt or formal subscription analytics because those tools and contexts are not evidenced in the CV.

The evidence bank is stronger on operational reporting, service improvement and performance control than on pure growth analytics, so I have framed fit around transferable measurement and leadership capability.
I used three examples only, because they give the strongest mix of reporting, customer outcome and scale-up discipline.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
